name: Macadamia-Nut Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
3. Toast macadamia nuts on a baking sheet until light brown (about 10 minutes). Let cool.
4. In a food processor, coarsely grind half of the nuts with one-quarter of the dry ingredients.
5. Cream butter and sugar in an electric mixer or by hand until light and fluffy.
6. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition. Mix in vanilla extract.
7. Stir in all nuts and ground nuts; add remaining flour mixture. Mix just until combined.
8. Form logs as described in the Almond Biscotti recipe (see p.35).
9. Bake at 350°F for about 30 minutes or until lightly golden but still soft. Remove from oven.
10. Reduce temperature to 300°F. Let cool on sheet for at least 10 minutes before slicing.
11. Cut into 3/4-inch slices and place flat in oven. Dry for 15 minutes or until firm.
12. Transfer to rack to cool. Store in airtight container.
